content:
# Joshua Chapter 4 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), page 228 | **Source Image:** `../../source-texts/hebrew-OT/JOS_004_page_001.jpg`

---

1 And it was, when all the nation had finished crossing the Yarden, and YHWH spoke to Yehoshua, saying:

2 "Take for yourselves from the people twelve men, one man, one man from [each] tribe.

3 And command them, saying: 'Take up for yourselves from here, from the middle of the Yarden, from the standing-place of the feet of the priests, firmly [set], twelve stones, and carry them over with you and set them down in the lodging-place where you shall lodge tonight.'"

4 And Yehoshua called to the twelve men whom he had prepared from the sons of Yisra'el, one man, one man from [each] tribe.

5 And Yehoshua said to them: "Cross over before the ark of YHWH your God into the middle of the Yarden, and lift up for yourselves each man one stone upon his shoulder, according to the number of the tribes of the sons of Yisra'el,

6 so that this shall be a sign in your midst. When your sons ask in time to come, saying: 'What [are] these stones to you?'

7 And you shall say to them: 'Because the waters of the Yarden were cut off before the ark of the covenant of YHWH; when it crossed into the Yarden the waters of the Yarden were cut off.' And these stones shall be for a memorial to the sons of Yisra'el unto eternity."

8 And the sons of Yisra'el did so, as Yehoshua commanded, and they took up twelve stones from the middle of the Yarden, as YHWH had spoken to Yehoshua, according to the number of the tribes of the sons of Yisra'el, and they carried them over with them to the lodging-place and set them down there.

9 And twelve stones Yehoshua set up in the middle of the Yarden, at the standing-place of the feet of the priests carrying the ark of the covenant; and they are there unto this day.

10 And the priests carrying the ark were standing in the middle of the Yarden until the finishing of every word that YHWH had commanded Yehoshua to speak to the people, according to all that Moshe had commanded Yehoshua. And the people hurried and crossed over.

11 And it was, when all the people had finished crossing, and the ark of YHWH crossed over, and the priests, before the people.

12 And the sons of Re'uven and the sons of Gad and the half-tribe of Menasheh crossed over armed before the sons of Yisra'el, as Moshe had spoken to them.

13 About forty thousand equipped for war crossed over before YHWH for battle, to the plains of Yericho.

14 On that day YHWH made Yehoshua great in the eyes of all Yisra'el, and they feared him as they had feared Moshe all the days of his life.

15 And YHWH spoke to Yehoshua, saying:

16 "Command the priests carrying the ark of the testimony, and let them come up from the Yarden."

17 And Yehoshua commanded the priests, saying: "Come up from the Yarden."

18 And it was, when the priests carrying the ark of the covenant of YHWH came up from the middle of the Yarden, [and] the soles of the feet of the priests were pulled out to the dry ground, and the waters of the Yarden returned to their place and went over all its banks as before.

19 And the people came up from the Yarden on the tenth of the first month, and they encamped in Gilgal on the eastern border of Yericho.

20 And those twelve stones which they had taken from the Yarden, Yehoshua set up in Gilgal.

21 And he spoke to the sons of Yisra'el, saying: "When your sons ask their fathers in time to come, saying: 'What [are] these stones?'

22 And you shall make known to your sons, saying: 'On dry ground Yisra'el crossed over this Yarden,

23 because YHWH your God dried up the waters of the Yarden from before you until you crossed over, as YHWH your God did to the Sea of Reeds, which he dried up from before us until we crossed over,

24 so that all the peoples of the earth shall know the hand of YHWH, that it [is] mighty, so that you shall fear YHWH your God all the days.'"

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v1 — Temporal clause structure:** The Hebrew וַיְהִי כַּאֲשֶׁר תַּמּוּ כָל־הַגּוֹי ("And it was when all the nation had finished") opens with the characteristic narrative wayehi construction. The term גּוֹי (goy, "nation") is used rather than עַם (am, "people"), a distinction preserved in this translation.

2. **v2 — Distributive repetition:** The Hebrew אִישׁ אֶחָד אִישׁ אֶחָד ("one man, one man") is a distributive idiom meaning "one man each." The repetition is preserved literally rather than smoothed to "one man from each tribe."

3. **v3 — הָכִין (hakhin):** Rendered "firmly [set]," from the root כּוּן (kun), describing the priests' feet standing firm in the riverbed. The Hiphil infinitive absolute indicates the established, fixed position.

4. **v4 — הֵכִין (hekhin):** "whom he had prepared" — same root כּוּן in the Hiphil perfect, indicating Yehoshua had previously designated these men (cf. 3:12).

5. **v9 — Two sets of stones:** This verse describes a second set of twelve stones erected *in* the Yarden itself at the priests' standing-place, distinct from the twelve carried to the camp (vv. 3, 8, 20). The Masoretic text preserves both traditions without harmonization.

6. **v10 — Moshe's command:** The phrase כְּכֹל אֲשֶׁר־צִוָּה מֹשֶׁה אֶת־יְהוֹשֻׁעַ ("according to all that Moshe had commanded Yehoshua") links the Jordan crossing to the Mosaic commission (Deut 31:7–8, 23).

7. **v12 — חֲמֻשִׁים (chamushim):** Rendered "armed" — this term (from חמשׁ) likely means "arrayed in battle order" or "armed in ranks of five." The same word appears in Exod 13:18 and Josh 1:14.

8. **v13 — אַרְבָעִים אֶלֶף (arba'im elef):** "About forty thousand" — the Hebrew uses the approximate particle כ (ke-) prefixed: כְּאַרְבָּעִים אֶלֶף. The number is rendered in words per EMET convention.

9. **v16 — הָעֵדוּת (ha'edut):** The ark is here called "the ark of the testimony" (אֲרוֹן הָעֵדֻת), whereas vv. 7, 9, 18 use "the ark of the covenant" (אֲרוֹן הַבְּרִית). The Masoretic variation is preserved without harmonization.

10. **v18 — נִתְּקוּ (nitequ):** "Were pulled out" — from the root נתק, meaning to pull away, to be torn loose. The verb conveys the dramatic moment when the priests' feet left the riverbed and the waters returned.

11. **v23 — Shift of person:** The Hebrew shifts from second person ("before you… until you crossed over") to first person ("before us until we crossed over"), collapsing the distance between the generation that crossed and future generations who will retell the story. This shift is preserved without correction.

12. **v24 — לְמַעַן דַּעַת (lema'an da'at):** The infinitive construct "so that… shall know" gives the theological purpose of the memorial stones: universal recognition of YHWH's power and Israel's ongoing reverence.

13. **LXX comparison (Vaticanus):** The Septuagint at Joshua 4 largely follows the Hebrew but expands v. 12 with additional detail on the Transjordanian tribes and uses κιβωτός τῆς διαθήκης κυρίου consistently where MT alternates between "ark of the covenant" and "ark of the testimony." The LXX also reads ἕξ ("six") for the number of stones in some variant traditions, though Vaticanus aligns with the MT's twelve.
